DARKSWORD. That's the name Google researchers gave to an iOS exploit chain active since late last year that chains six vulnerabilities in iOS and Safari to take over your iPhone with a single website visit.  No app download. No phishing click. Just a compromised page.  The affected range is iOS 18.4 through 18.7. If you haven't updated, you're exposed right now.  WHAT DARKSWORD DOES  The attack is what researchers call a drive-by. Your device loads a malicious or compromised site. The exploit chain fires automatically. Malware executes. You never knew it happened.  Multiple actors are already using it. Commercial spyware vendors. State-backed groups. Campaigns confirmed in Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine. In Ukraine, attackers hit at least two websites including a government site. In Saudi Arabia, a fake Snapchat lookalike was the delivery vehicle.  GHOSTBLADE: THE PAYLOAD  The malware deployed in the Ukrainian campaign is called Ghostblade. It's JavaScript-based and it moves fast.  One sweep. It pulls SMS and iMessage content, call history, contacts, Wi-Fi credentials, Safari cookies and browsing history, location data, notes, calendar entries, health data, photos, iCloud Drive files, SIM info, emails, installed app list, saved passwords, and message history from Telegram and WhatsApp.  Then it goes after crypto. Coinbase. Binance. Kraken. Kucoin. OKX. Mexc. Ledger. Trezor. Metamask. Exodus. Uniswap. Phantom. Gnosis Safe. If you have any of those installed, it notes them. Direct theft and profile building for future targeting.  Then Ghostblade wipes itself. No traces. Most victims will never know it happened.  This isn't long-term surveillance. It's a precision smash-and-grab.  WHAT TO DO RIGHT NOW  UPDATE YOUR IPHONE. This is not optional. Apple patched CVE-2026-20700 and related vulnerabilities. If you're still on 18.4 through 18.7, you are a valid target.  If you're a journalist, activist, or anyone with access to sensitive data or systems, enable Lockdown Mode. Settings > Privacy & Security > Lockdown Mode > Turn On. It degrades usability but it has a documented track record against targeted attacks at this level.  Beyond that:  Move high-value crypto to a hardware wallet. Mobile wallets are for small balances only. Enable MFA on every exchange and financial account, preferably FIDO2 hardware keys or an authenticator app. Stolen passwords alone should not be enough to clean out an account. Stop auto-filling credentials on high-risk sites. Review app permissions. Location, Photos, Contacts, Microphone, Camera, Health. Revoke anything you don't actively need. Don't follow links from unsolicited messages, especially for Snapchat, crypto platforms, banking, or anything financial.  THE BIGGER POINT  DarkSword is already in the hands of both commercial spyware vendors and nation-state actors. That combination means the targeting criteria are wide. Corporate executives, government employees, crypto holders, journalists, veterans with clearances, and small business owners are all in scope depending on who bought the tool.  The people who walk away clean from situations like this are the ones who kept their attack surface small before it mattered. Updates. MFA. Hardware wallets. Minimal permissions. Basic posture.  This is what security hygiene actually buys you. Not paranoia. Not a false sense of safety. A narrower target profile. ‼️🇦🇪 A threat actor using the handle "rSosa" is selling reconnaissance data and access points related to the Department of Government Enablement (DGE) in Abu Dhabi on a dark web forum. The actor claims to have spent a week mapping the department's "Shadow AI stack," describing exposed development portals and internal AI infrastructure.  The listing highlights several findings including 3 live Flowise & LiteLLM instances reportedly serving as backends for GovGPT, a training portal running on end-of-life PHP 7.4.33 vulnerable to remote code execution, Liferay DXP instances with local authentication misconfigurations requiring no SSO, and full Envoy/Nuxt header mappings allegedly bypassing security measures. Sample data was provided as a code snippet to demonstrate the filtered reconnaissance.

‼️🇪🇸 A threat actor using the handle "fanfan" has leaked the mailing list database of comerciodomorrazo.com, a Spanish commerce website.  The breach includes a single CSV file containing 15,938 records of subscriber data. sample_tweets.text: This is a great demo. The know-it-all contrarian replies make me cringe.  Here’s a quick explainer what’s going on AND why it’s much worse than it appears.   Copper Clad Aluminum (CCA) cables are copper cables with aluminum cores. They exist purely for the ~25% cost reduction. They almost always have cost-cutting insulation. Let’s touch on both topics, then combine into a unified nightmare :)  CCA vs solid copper: CCA has lots of negatives. The primary in this context is that it has ~60% more electrical resistance. This degrades high speed signals(2.5G/5G/10G), especially as you get near the limits of length. It also significantly increases how much the cable heat up. Let’s say you are doing PoE++ at 100W, across 100 meters, on a tiny 24 bundle pair. The CCA will produce ~150W of additional heat. Thats a small space heater! But it’s not evenly spread across the cable because…  CCA is also brittle, so any partial hairline cracks will become concentrated hot spots. CCA also doesn’t “bite” well when terminating, which creates another opportunity for hot spots. Worse: this gets worse over time due to aluminum oxidation.   And then we get to the insulation. Flame retardant and stiffeners cost money, so they are left out. That means you have insulation that gets very soft under much lower temperatures on a metal that gets much warmer. That the metal starts to squeeze together which will mess with the data signal, or worst case, allowing the start of a short (another hot spot). Keep in mind, these cables are often run in bundles (compounding the heat) and through hot locations like attics.   And if the insulation ever catches fire, it does NOT self extinguish. Yes, the cable itself could ignite, but ANYTHING starts a fire, you effectively have a fuse inside your walls now. That’s exactly what @TracketPacer is showing in this video.   Lots of commercial buildings have “fire walls” that stop the spread of fire between sections of the building. One of the few things allowed to pass through these fire walls is… data cables!   So to save 25% on your cabling, you are accepting a big degradation in data performance which will often lead to lots of debugging. It drops the voltage for PoE equipment… more debugging! Anytime the bundles are disturbed in any way… good chance of more debugging! And then you have the fire safety hazard and code violations that can leave you holding the bag for the consequences instead of the insurance.   STOP BUYING CHEAP CABLES!

최근 공격자들은 AI 기반의 자동화된 취약점 탐색 및 공격 도구를 적극적으로 활용하며, 특히 CI/CD 파이프라인과 클라우드 인프라의 설정 오류를 노리는 공급망 공격이 급증하고 있습니다. 또한, 지정학적 갈등과 연계된 Wiper 악성코드 배포가 활발하며, 초기 침투부터 데이터 탈취까지의 시간이 22초 이내로 단축되는 등 공격 속도가 극도로 빨라졌습니다. 더불어, EDR 우회 기술과 합법적인 관리 도구(VS Code, GSocket 등)를 악용하는 Living-off-the-land(LotL) 기법이 고도화되고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **WaterPlum (TheHackerNews):** VS Code Auto-Run Tasks를 악용하여 StoatWaffle 악성코드를 배포하는 북한 배후의 위협 행위자.
*   **TeamPCP (BleepingComputer):** Kubernetes 환경을 타겟으로 하며, 특정 시스템(Iran 설정)을 감지하여 데이터를 파괴하는 Wiper 악성코드 유포.
*   **Void Manticore / Handala Hack (Check Point):** 이란 배후의 해킹 그룹으로, 데이터 파괴형 Wiper 공격과 'Hack and Leak' 전략을 병행.
*   **Larva-26002 (AhnLab ASEC):** 관리 부실 MS-SQL 서버를 대상으로 침투하여 스캐너 설치 및 랜섬웨어(Trigona, Mimic) 유포.
*   **Tycoon2FA (BleepingComputer):** 경찰의 단속 이후에도 즉시 복구되어 활동 중인 Phishing-as-a-Service(PhaaS) 플랫폼.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **CipherForce:** 대한민국 취업 포털을 대상으로 사이버 공격을 주장하는 신규 위협 행위자 확인 (AhnLab ASEC).
*   **국내 APT 동향:** 2026년 2월 기준 국내 타겟 APT 공격이 지속적으로 모니터링되고 있으며, 특히 기업 및 기관 대상의 피싱 및 악성코드 유포가 활발함 (AhnLab ASEC).
*   **정부 정책:** 과기정통부 주관 사이버보안 펀드 확대 및 AI 바우처 지원사업 등 차세대 보안 기술 육성 정책 추진 (데일리시큐).

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-3055:** NetScaler ADC/Gateway, CVSS 9.3, 인증되지 않은 원격 공격자에 의한 메모리 정보 노출 및 악용 가능.
*   **CVE-2026-4681:** PTC Windchill & FlexPLM, CVSS 9.3, Java 역직렬화(Deserialization)를 통한 RCE.
*   **CVE-2026-32913:** OpenClaw, CVSS 9.8(추정), 부적절한 헤더 검증으로 인한 SSRF 및 권한 탈취.
*   **CVE-2026-20131:** Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center, CVSS 9.0(추정), 원격 코드 실행(RCE) 취약점.
*   **CVE-2026-27198:** Formwork CMS, CVSS 8.8, 편집자 권한을 관리자 권한으로 승격 가능한 취약점.

*   **CI/CD 환경 점검:** Trivy 등 보안 도구의 설정 및 CI/CD 파이프라인 내 자격 증명(SSH keys, Cloud tokens) 노출 여부를 즉시 감사하십시오.
*   **NetScaler 및 Cisco 장비 패치:** CVE-2026-3055 및 CVE-2026-20131에 대한 벤더사 최신 보안 패치를 즉각 적용하십시오.
*   **MS-SQL 서버 보안 강화:** 외부 노출된 MS-SQL 서버의 접근 제어(ACL)를 강화하고, 불필요한 스캐너 설치 여부를 확인하십시오.
